title: Iron status in endurance athletes
source: European Society of Medicine review; PMC case studies; USA Triathlon
text: Iron deficiency is common in endurance athletes, affecting roughly 15 to 35 percent of female athletes. Training adds to iron losses through foot-strike impact, sweat and the gut, and that sits on top of menstrual losses. Iron deficiency without anaemia, where ferritin is low but haemoglobin still looks normal, is easy to miss, yet it can already blunt endurance performance and the body's adaptation to training. Because of this, sports medicine often aims for a higher ferritin level in athletes than in the general population.
